# Questions in Livestream 17

Live Stream #17: Back to Prime Time

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=155" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35</a> | Did the Egyptians have a rich music culture, and what instruments did they prefer? | Yes. He highlighted flutes, sistra, cymbals, and large harp-like instruments as especially important. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=290" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:50</a> | Who might the historical Nimrod have been? | He said he had not studied the question enough to decide, though Sargon and Naram-Sin are common candidates. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=395" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">6:35</a> | What were the Urim and Thummim, and where did the temple candelabrum come from? | He tentatively favored Heiser's dice-based theory for the Urim and Thummim and said the menorah's deeper antecedents remain uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=716" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:56</a> | If cherubim and seraphim use imagery connected to Egyptian gods, what does that say theologically about Yahweh? | It emphasizes Yahweh's absolute holiness and uniqueness, because the symbolism is redirected toward a God who needs no idol or human care. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=948" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:48</a> | How do you interpret God hardening Pharaoh's heart in an Egyptian context? Did God take away Pharaoh's free will, and where can someone read more? | He said Exodus first shows Pharaoh hardening his own heart, then God confirms him in the path he freely chose. He did not name a specific reading source. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=1106" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:26</a> | Who were the Nephilim and the Rephaim? | He leaned toward viewing the Nephilim as the offspring of a fallen priestly institution, but said he needed more work before speaking confidently about the Rephaim. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=1335" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:15</a> | Has Jeremiah 19:6 about the Valley of Hinnom been fulfilled already, and is it about hell? | He said Jeremiah is speaking first about the literal valley and its history, with the later hell imagery arising as a later idealization. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=1655" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:35</a> | Why are some cherubim in Ezekiel and Revelation described as having three animal heads and one human head, and where do the ophanim come from? | He treated the animal imagery as analogical language for spiritual beings and, after clarification, connected the wheels with eyes to God's portable throne imagery. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=2168" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">36:08</a> | Do the priestly garments in Leviticus come from somewhere in the ancient Near East? | Some elements do, but he said the full high-priestly outfit does not map neatly onto Egyptian parallels and may reflect a more distinct synthesis. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=2425" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">40:25</a> | Does Egyptian connect breath, wind, and spirit the way Hebrew and Greek do? | Only partially. Breath is tied to life, but Egyptian divides personal spiritual functions differently through the ka and ba. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=2631" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:51</a> | Why would copyists of the Old Testament leave numbers unchanged after their cultural context became distant? | He said reverence for the received text usually kept scribes from updating numbers or place names, though some changes did occur and can complicate interpretation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=3029" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:29</a> | What books would he recommend on Arabian polytheism or Amorite beliefs? | He said the literature exists but lies outside his specialty, so he could only point generally toward work on old Arabian history and Mesopotamian sources. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=3290" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:50</a> | Do the huge reign lengths in Sumerian and Babylonian king lists show that humans really lived hundreds of thousands of years ago? | No. He treated those figures as idiomatic or schematic rather than literal chronological records. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=3683" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:23</a> | Could the Tempest Stela refer to the Thera eruption? | Possibly, but he said the dating problems around Thera are still too unresolved to be confident. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=3818" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:38</a> | Why does he dislike archaeoastronomy so much? | Because the observations are too unstable, too assumption-heavy, and often ask the evidence to do jobs it was never meant to do. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=4328" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:08</a> | What was the nature of the spirit breathed in for the Egyptians? | He clarified that breath was life for Egyptians, while personal spiritual faculties were divided between the ka and ba rather than identified with breath itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=4466" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:26</a> | Does Genesis 13:10's comparison to "the land of Egypt" reflect an Exodus perspective? | Yes. He said it likely reflects a later Exodus-aware frame rather than a neutral geographical aside. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=4745" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:19:05</a> | Who is his least favorite ruler of Egypt? | Hatshepsut. He described her as an opportunistic usurper and gave a strongly negative character reading of her reign. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=5033" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:53</a> | Is the tree in Eden related to Asherah, or is it Mesopotamian? | He said the Eden tree belongs to a broader ancient Near Eastern tree symbolism and that Asherah is more closely comparable to an Osiris-style cult object. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=5376" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:36</a> | How common was it for generals like Horemheb to become ruler of Egypt? | Not common, but not unique either. He stressed that Egyptian kingship was fundamentally military in character. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVpJZyvwIq8?t=5603" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:33:23</a> | How should someone balance knowledge and spirituality without leaning too much on their own understanding? | He rejected the split between them and said scholarship can itself be an act of worship when kept humble under God's word. |
